## Overall Insight

Once positivity Q(v,v) > 0 is established for one nonzero generator v of T_{2g}, G-equivariance of Q and irreducibility of T_{2g} (a 3-dim real irrep of the octahedral group Oh) together with Schur's lemma imply Q = lambda * Id on T_{2g} with lambda > 0, giving positive definiteness.

### Subproblem 5: Lift positivity from one generator to all of T_{2g}

**Statement**: Let Q be the quadratic form Q(w,w) = Hess_p log J_8(w,w) on V = (direct sum T_{p_i} S^2) / SO(3), let T_{2g} be the irreducible 3-dimensional G-subrepresentation generated by v = (v_i), and assume Q(v,v) > 0 for one nonzero v in T_{2g}. Prove Q is positive definite on all of T_{2g}.

**Role**: Upgrades the sign computation for one generator to positive definiteness on the whole irreducible summand.

**Approach**: Choose a G-invariant inner product on T_{2g}. Represent Q|_{T_{2g}} by a self-adjoint G-equivariant endomorphism L via Q(w,w') = <Lw,w'>. By Schur's lemma (T_{2g} is an absolutely irreducible real representation of G), L = lambda * Id. Since Q(v,v) = lambda*|v|^2 > 0, lambda > 0.

**Note on Schur's lemma for real representations**: For a real absolutely irreducible representation of a finite group, the only G-equivariant endomorphisms are real scalar multiples of the identity. T_{2g} is absolutely irreducible (it remains irreducible over C), so Schur applies over R.

## Integration Sketch

Subproblem 5 is the final step. With I < 0 from Subproblem 4, Q(v,v) = -16*pi^2 * I > 0. Schur's lemma then gives Q = lambda*Id on T_{2g} with lambda > 0, completing the proof that the Hessian is positive definite on T_{2g}.
